svg.text: Where each control fires — and where it leaves a gap. The diagonal is what defense-in-depth covers; the white space is the attacker's playground.

g.text: Input classifiers (Llama Guard, Constitutional, Prompt Shields)
Multimodal input guards (OCR scan, audio length cap)
Reliable coverage (>80% reduction in ASR vs. unprotected baseline)
Partial coverage (helps but bypassable; relies on pattern recognition)
No row of dots is fully filled. The white space is the residual risk —
attacks that get past every layer in the stack.
Cover the diagonal first (training × direct attacks; architecture × agent attacks),

svg.text: Practical takeaway: pair adversarial training (rows 1–2) with input/output classifiers (rows 3, 5) for the conversational surface, and architectural isolation
(row 6) plus HITL (row 7) for the agent surface. No single defense covers the lower-right (encoding/multimodal/IPI) — this is where most production failures land.

Synthesis from: Anthropic Constitutional Classifiers (2025–2026); Llama Guard 2 (Meta 2024); Microsoft Spotlighting (Hines et al. 2024); CaMeL (Debenedetti et al. 2025); StruQ (Chen et al. 2024); Circuit Breakers (Zou et al. 2024).
